name: Prosperity Indiana (Indiana Association for Community Economic Development)
description_short: Statewide nonprofit membership organization providing advocacy, training, and resources to community economic development practitioners across Indiana since 1986.
description_long:
Prosperity Indiana, officially the Indiana Association for Community Economic Development, was founded in 1986 as a statewide membership organization dedicated to strengthening Hoosier communities through economic development initiatives. The organization has grown to approximately 200 member organizations representing roughly 1,100 community economic development practitioners from public, private, and nonprofit sectors across Indiana.

Prosperity Indiana offers a comprehensive suite of free and member services including housing counseling, training programs, consulting resources, policy advocacy, and coalition-building initiatives. Their flagship programs include the Assets & Opportunity Network, Hoosiers for Responsible Lending coalition, Hoosier Housing Needs Coalition, Housing First Toolkit, Community Loan Center support, and the Housing4Hoosiers initiative. They provide training sessions such as Housing Navigation 101, maintain an outcomes platform for tracking progress, and facilitate regional meetings and annual summits for member engagement and professional development.

The organization distinguishes itself through its comprehensive advocacy platform, including a dedicated Advocacy Action Center and legislative engagement resources. Rather than providing direct consumer services, Prosperity Indiana strengthens the capacity of member organizations to serve communities. Their focus on social determinants of health, inclusive growth, and financial capability building demonstrates a commitment to systemic community economic development rather than transactional services. They maintain transparent governance with published board information and financial disclosures.

As a membership and advocacy organization, Prosperity Indiana is best suited for community development professionals, nonprofit organizations, and practitioners seeking training, networking, and policy influence. Individual consumers should not contact Prosperity Indiana for direct financial assistance; instead, they should seek help through member organizations or the specific programs and coalitions Prosperity Indiana coordinates.
pros: 200+ member organizations representing 1,100 practitioners provides extensive community development network across Indiana
Offers free training programs including Housing Navigation 101 series with multiple sessions
Maintains policy advocacy infrastructure including Advocacy Action Center and legislative resource guides
Coordinates multiple coalitions focused on specific issues (responsible lending, housing needs, asset building)
Provides consulting and technical assistance to strengthen member organizational capacity
Founded in 1986 with 40+ year track record serving Indiana communities
Transparent governance with published board, staff, and financial information
cons: Membership organization focused on practitioner capacity-building, not direct consumer services
No direct financial assistance, counseling, or loans available to individual consumers
Limited public-facing programs; most resources require organizational membership or affiliation
Website navigation complex with emphasis on internal member portal and engagement rather than consumer education
No apparent 24/7 consumer support or rapid response mechanisms for individuals in financial crisis
best_for: Community development organizations and nonprofits seeking professional training and peer networks
Public sector practitioners implementing community economic development initiatives
Policy advocates and organizations working on affordable housing and responsible lending issues
Member organizations looking for technical assistance and capacity-building resources
diagnosis: Prosperity Indiana is best for community development professionals, nonprofit practitioners, and organizations seeking training, policy influence, and peer networks—not for individual consumers seeking direct financial help. Individuals needing financial assistance should contact member organizations directly or access specific programs like Housing4Hoosiers rather than Prosperity Indiana itself.
services: Housing Navigation 101 training program (multi-session virtual format)
Housing counseling resources and training for member organizations
Consulting services to strengthen organizational capacity
Policy advocacy and legislative engagement through Advocacy Action Center
Community Loan Center support and coordination
Membership and networking opportunities through regional meetings
Annual Summit for professional development and coalition building
Coalition support including Hoosiers for Responsible Lending and Hoosier Housing Needs Coalition
Outcomes tracking platform for member organizations
